notes: Alcohol can be brought into the UK for personal use subject to origin-based limits
Some food can be brought into the UK but many animal products and fresh items are restricted
You can bring large amounts of cash into the UK, but amounts over a set threshold must be declared.
Duty-free allowances apply when entering the UK, covering alcohol, tobacco, and other goods within set limits.
You can bring personal electronics into the UK, but high-value items may need to be declared.
You can bring medication into the UK for personal use, but controlled drugs have stricter requirements.
You can bring tobacco into the UK for personal use within allowance limits.
Most weapons are prohibited from being brought into the UK, even in checked luggage.
Prescription drugs can be brought into the UK if they are for personal use and properly documented.
You must declare certain goods when entering the UK, especially if they exceed allowances or are restricted.
